Josh Shapiro.overall_note: Modeled from public actions as governor, prior campaign platform, official state actions, and reputable reporting.

positions.summary: Opposes major Medicaid cuts and has warned that federal cuts could reduce coverage and harm rural hospitals.
Has supported protecting abortion access and ended state funding for Real Alternatives, an anti-abortion counseling network.

sources.label: Josh Shapiro profile — abortion access / Real Alternatives

positions.summary: Implemented automatic voter registration for eligible Pennsylvanians at DMV interactions.

sources.label: Josh Shapiro profile — automatic voter registration

positions.summary: Has supported hiring additional police officers and emphasized having more officers on the beat.

sources.label: Josh Shapiro profile — police hiring proposal

positions.summary: Modeled as supportive of public education funding, while noting he has also shown openness to private-school voucher/scholarship proposals.

sources.label: Wall Street Journal — Shapiro and school vouchers

positions.summary: Modeled as moderate clean-energy support, with some skepticism toward more aggressive climate-market approaches such as RGGI.

sources.label: Josh Shapiro profile — energy and RGGI

positions.summary: Modeled as economically mixed: supports job creation, workforce development, and has supported corporate tax reduction.

sources.label: Josh Shapiro profile — corporate tax and workforce

Stacy Garrity.overall_note: Modeled from campaign launch reporting, public support for national Republican fiscal/border legislation, record as Pennsylvania Treasurer, and Trump-aligned campaign positioning.

positions.summary: Campaign positioning emphasizes inflation, lower costs, spending restraint, and putting money back in taxpayers' pockets.

sources.label: Stacy Garrity profile — support for OBBB framing
